clusters.title: After ICE fatally shot multiple people, officials speak out: "We want them out"
clusters.neutral_headline: Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, NPR News and others

items.title: After ICE fatally shot multiple people, officials speak out: "We want them out"
items.standfirst: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials shot and killed two men in the last week in Maine and in Texas. Maine's House speaker, a Houston prosecutor and a Minnesota attorney spoke with CBS News about the violence.
items.content: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials shot and killed two men in the last week in Maine and in Texas. Maine's House speaker, a Houston prosecutor and a Minnesota attorney spoke with CBS News about the violence.

items.title: Security video captures moments after man was fatally shot by ICE in Maine
items.standfirst: A home security camera captured the sound of five gunshots when Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, a Colombian national, was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer.
items.content: A home security camera captured the sound of five gunshots when Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, a Colombian national, was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer.

items.title: ICE halts most vehicle stops following deadly shootings, sources say
items.standfirst: Neither of the victims of the ICE shootings in Maine or Texas were the target of enforcement operations, according to the Department of Homeland Security.
items.content: Neither of the victims of the ICE shootings in Maine or Texas were the target of enforcement operations, according to the Department of Homeland Security.

items.title: 7/14: CBS Evening News
items.standfirst: More than 20 inches of rain are forecasted for parts of Texas; Immigration and Customs Enforcement changes its tactics after deadly shootings.
items.content: More than 20 inches of rain are forecasted for parts of Texas; Immigration and Customs Enforcement changes its tactics after deadly shootings.

items.title: Trump administration orders ICE to halt traffic stops after Maine shooting, US media reports
items.standfirst: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) suspended the use of traffic stops to detain immigrants across the country, US media reported Tuesday, a day after an ICE agent shot and killed a Colombian driver in Maine. The fatal shooting came a week after an ICE officer killed a motorist in Texas, renewing criticism of the agency's enforcement operations.
items.content: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) suspended the use of traffic stops to detain immigrants across the country, US media reported Tuesday, a day after an ICE agent shot and killed a Colombian driver in Maine. The fatal shooting came a week after an ICE officer killed a motorist in Texas, renewing criticism of the agency's enforcement operations.

items.title: In the aftermath of deadly shootings, ICE pauses most traffic stops
items.standfirst: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement will pause non-urgent vehicle stops after two deadly shootings in less than a week, Maine U.S. Sen. Angus King's office tells NPR.
items.content: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement will pause non-urgent vehicle stops after two deadly shootings in less than a week, Maine U.S. Sen. Angus King's office tells NPR.(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)

items.title: Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine
items.standfirst: It comes less than a week after an undocumented migrant was fatally shot in Houston by an immigration agent.
items.content: It comes less than a week after an undocumented migrant was fatally shot in Houston by an immigration agent.

clusters.title: World Cup 2026: Which team are supporters from eliminated nations rooting for?
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain fans erupt as team reaches World Cup final
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, CBS News, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: World Cup 2026: Which team are supporters from eliminated nations rooting for?
items.standfirst: During the France-Spain semi-final, many supporters at the FIFA fan zone in Dallas were wearing jerseys from nations that got eliminated earlier in the competition. We went to ask them which team they were supporting now.
items.content: During the France-Spain semi-final, many supporters at the FIFA fan zone in Dallas were wearing jerseys from nations that got eliminated earlier in the competition. We went to ask them which team they were supporting now.

items.title: Spain defeats France 2-0 to reach FIFA World Cup final
items.standfirst: Spain emerged triumphant from its World Cup semifinal on Tuesday, beating France 2-0 and securing a spot in the final, which will be played in New Jersey on Sunday. CBS News' Nicole Valdes reports.
items.content: Spain emerged triumphant from its World Cup semifinal on Tuesday, beating France 2-0 and securing a spot in the final, which will be played in New Jersey on Sunday. CBS News' Nicole Valdes reports.

items.title: Spain fans erupt as team reaches World Cup final
items.standfirst: Fans erupted in celebration after Spain beat France 2-0 in the semifinals, securing their place in the 2026 Finals
items.content: Fans erupted in celebration after Spain beat France 2-0 in the semifinals, securing their place in the 2026 Finals

items.title: “We’ve given the French a proper trashing": the Spaniards delighted, the French disappointed
items.standfirst: “We’ve given the French a proper trashing.” In Dallas, Spanish fans were delighted, whilst French fans were disappointed, following Spain’s 2-0 victory over France in the World Cup semi-final.
items.content: “We’ve given the French a proper trashing.” In Dallas, Spanish fans were delighted, whilst French fans were disappointed, following Spain’s 2-0 victory over France in the World Cup semi-final.

items.title: World Cup 2026: In Dallas, Spanish supporters celebrate their team's victory over France
items.standfirst: Spain have just won their World Cup semi-final against France. In the FIFA fan zone in Dallas, Spanish supporters celebrated this 2-0 victory.
items.content: Spain have just won their World Cup semi-final against France. In the FIFA fan zone in Dallas, Spanish supporters celebrated this 2-0 victory.

items.title: France's World Cup dream extinguished in chastening semi-final loss to Spain
items.standfirst: Didier Deschamps' France were hot favourites to lift the famous golden trophy after a string of breathtaking attacking displays at the World Cup. But they met their match in the semi-finals when faced with slick European champions Spain, who outplayed and outsmarted Les Bleus to deny them a third consecutive final.
items.content: Didier Deschamps' France were hot favourites to lift the famous golden trophy after a string of breathtaking attacking displays at the World Cup. But they met their match in the semi-finals when faced with slick European champions Spain, who outplayed and outsmarted Les Bleus to deny them a third consecutive final.

items.title: Spain deliver masterclass to beat France 2-0 and reach World Cup final
items.standfirst: European champions Spain beat France with controlled display to book final against Argentina or England.
items.content: European champions Spain beat France with controlled display to book final against Argentina or England.

items.title: Spain into World Cup final with victory over France
items.standfirst: Spain are into the 2026 World Cup final after goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro see them convincingly beat France 2-0 in their semi-final in Dallas.
items.content: Spain are into the 2026 World Cup final after goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro see them convincingly beat France 2-0 in their semi-final in Dallas.

items.title: Spain deliver masterclass to sink French Armada and reach World Cup final
items.standfirst: European champions Spain produced a technical and tactical masterclass to defeat World Cup favourites France at the Dallas Stadium on Tuesday, scoring a goal in each half as they tamed Les Bleus' attacking armada to book a place in the final, where they will face either England or Argentina.
items.content: European champions Spain produced a technical and tactical masterclass to defeat World Cup favourites France at the Dallas Stadium on Tuesday, scoring a goal in each half as they tamed Les Bleus' attacking armada to book a place in the final, where they will face either England or Argentina.

items.title: Brilliant Spain defeat toothless France to reach World Cup final
items.standfirst: Spain tamed France's galaxy of stars at the Dallas Stadium to reach the World Cup final for the first time since their 2010 triumph, powering past Les Bleus 2-0 with goals by Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro. They will face either England or Argentina in the July 19 final.
items.content: Spain tamed France's galaxy of stars at the Dallas Stadium to reach the World Cup final for the first time since their 2010 triumph, powering past Les Bleus 2-0 with goals by Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro. They will face either England or Argentina in the July 19 final.

clusters.title: World Cup: French dream comes to an end against dominating Spain
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain crashes Paris party by beating France in the World Cup semis
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Al Jazeera, NPR News

items.title: World Cup: French dream comes to an end against dominating Spain
items.standfirst: The French team were defeated by Spain (2–0) in the World Cup semi-final. France’s dream of reaching a third consecutive final – a feat achieved by only two other nations before them – has come to an end.
items.content: The French team were defeated by Spain (2–0) in the World Cup semi-final. France’s dream of reaching a third consecutive final – a feat achieved by only two other nations before them – has come to an end.

items.title: Spain crashes Paris party by beating France in the World Cup semis
items.standfirst: Dreams of reaching the World Cup final for a third time running were dashed when France were beaten 2-0 by Spain.
items.content: Dreams of reaching the World Cup final for a third time running were dashed when France were beaten 2-0 by Spain.

items.title: Spain sparkles to shock France 2-0 and advance to the World Cup final
items.standfirst: Spain is back in the men's World Cup final for the first time since 2010. That's also when Spain won its only World Cup title. France had hoped to reach the final for the third straight tournament.
items.content: Spain is back in the men's World Cup final for the first time since 2010. That's also when Spain won its only World Cup title. France had hoped to reach the final for the third straight tournament.(Image credit: Paul Ellis)

clusters.title: U.S. restarts blockade and strikes Iran as Trump seeks control of Strait of Hormuz
clusters.neutral_headline: US military reimposes naval blockade on Iranian ports, launches new strikes
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, NBC World

items.title: U.S. restarts blockade and strikes Iran as Trump seeks control of Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: As the U.S. restarts its Iran blockade and carries out more strikes, President Trump has called off a planned 20% fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: As the U.S. restarts its Iran blockade and carries out more strikes, President Trump has called off a planned 20% fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: US military reimposes naval blockade on Iranian ports, launches new strikes
items.standfirst: US forces on Tuesday reimposed a naval blockade to prevent ships from sailing to or from Iran's ports and conducted strikes against the country for a fourth day in response to Tehran's attacks on commercial ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz and on Gulf facilities.
items.content: US forces on Tuesday reimposed a naval blockade to prevent ships from sailing to or from Iran's ports and conducted strikes against the country for a fourth day in response to Tehran's attacks on commercial ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz and on Gulf facilities.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will launch an 'Iranian blockade' in the Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Trump said the U.S. will reinstate a naval blockade on Iranian ships in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing attacks between the U.S. and Iran. NBC News' Matt Bradley reports on the ongoing power struggle for control of the strait.
items.content: President Trump said the U.S. will reinstate a naval blockade on Iranian ships in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing attacks between the U.S. and Iran. NBC News' Matt Bradley reports on the ongoing power struggle for control of the strait.

items.title: Oil surges more than 9% after Trump reimposes Iran blockade
items.standfirst: Trump said the U.S. “will be reimbursed” for helping ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Crude oil hit its highest price in about a month.
items.content: Trump said the U.S. “will be reimbursed” for helping ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Crude oil hit its highest price in about a month.

clusters.title: Supreme Court Justices give chilling accounts of threats to their safety
clusters.neutral_headline: Supreme court justices request $14.6m increase in security amid rise in threats
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Supreme Court Justices give chilling accounts of threats to their safety
items.standfirst: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett spoke with lawmakers about funding for security and other needs for the court on Tuesday.
items.content: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett spoke with lawmakers about funding for security and other needs for the court on Tuesday.(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)

items.title: Supreme court justices request $14.6m increase in security amid rise in threats
items.standfirst: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett appear before House lawmakers in rare testimony to ask for more protectionSupreme court justice Amy Coney Barrett told House lawmakers that a sharp rise in threats against her and other justices is increasingly affecting her personal and family lives.Barrett and fellow supreme court justice Elena Kagan made the case for increased security in rare House testimony to discuss the court’s budget request. The last time a sitting justice answered questions on Capitol Hill was 2019. Continue reading...
items.content: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett appear before House lawmakers in rare testimony to ask for more protectionSupreme court justice Amy Coney Barrett told House lawmakers that a sharp rise in threats against her and other justices is increasingly affecting her personal and family lives.Barrett and fellow supreme court justice Elena Kagan made the case for increased security in rare House testimony to discuss the court’s budget request. The last time a sitting justice answered questions on Capitol Hill was 2019. Continue reading...

items.title: Supreme Court Justices Barrett and Kagan discuss security funding on Capitol Hill
items.standfirst: Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett met with lawmakers on Tuesday for a regular budget hearing.
items.content: Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett met with lawmakers on Tuesday for a regular budget hearing.

items.title: Supreme Court pushes Congress for more security: "Threats have come very close"
items.standfirst: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett urged Congress to provide additional funding to enhance protection for the justices as they face a rise in threats.
items.content: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett urged Congress to provide additional funding to enhance protection for the justices as they face a rise in threats.

clusters.title: US Congress takes next step to make daylight saving time permanent
clusters.neutral_headline: US Congress takes next step to make daylight saving time permanent
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: US Congress takes next step to make daylight saving time permanent
items.standfirst: The House of Representatives passed a Trump-backed bill that would end decades of Americans having to reset their clocks twice a year.
items.content: The House of Representatives passed a Trump-backed bill that would end decades of Americans having to reset their clocks twice a year.

items.title: Bill to make daylight saving time permanent passes US House vote: ‘Americans are tired of the clock change’
items.standfirst: Sunshine Protection Act has bipartisan support, including backing of Trump and some Democratic co-sponsors, and will head to the Senate nextThe US House of Representatives on Tuesday voted overwhelmingly to approve a bill that would end the practice of changing clocks twice a year and make daylight saving time permanent.The bid to end clock-changing, dubbed the Sunshine Protection Act, has bipartisan support, including the backing of Donald Trump and some Democratic co-sponsors. Following the 308-117 tally in the House, the bill next goes to the Senate. Continue reading...
items.content: Sunshine Protection Act has bipartisan support, including backing of Trump and some Democratic co-sponsors, and will head to the Senate nextThe US House of Representatives on Tuesday voted overwhelmingly to approve a bill that would end the practice of changing clocks twice a year and make daylight saving time permanent.The bid to end clock-changing, dubbed the Sunshine Protection Act, has bipartisan support, including the backing of Donald Trump and some Democratic co-sponsors. Following the 308-117 tally in the House, the bill next goes to the Senate. Continue reading...

items.title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
items.standfirst: The measure also allows states to opt out if they take action before the federal law is enacted.
items.content: The measure also allows states to opt out if they take action before the federal law is enacted.

items.title: Wife describes moment husband nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane
items.standfirst: A man was nearly sucked head-first out of a cabin window mid-air on a Ryanair plane.
items.content: A man was nearly sucked head-first out of a cabin window mid-air on a Ryanair plane.

items.title: 'If we die, we die together': Wife of man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane speaks of ordeal
items.standfirst: Svetlana Grković told Serbian media that her husband is "seriously injured and in shock".
items.content: Svetlana Grković told Serbian media that her husband is "seriously injured and in shock".

items.title: Wife of passenger sucked out of plane window describes their ordeal
items.standfirst: A woman whose husband was partially sucked out of the window during a Ryanair flight has described how half of his body was left sticking out of the plane for "up to two minutes".
items.content: A woman whose husband was partially sucked out of the window during a Ryanair flight has described how half of his body was left sticking out of the plane for "up to two minutes".

clusters.title: Mahmoud Khalil alleges conspiracy in lawsuit against Trump officials
clusters.neutral_headline: Mahmoud Khalil alleges conspiracy in lawsuit against Trump officials
bullet_summary: The lawsuit alleges senior Trump administration officials, pro-Israel group the Heritage Foundation and online surveillance groups Canary Mission and Betar conspired against him

items.title: Mahmoud Khalil alleges conspiracy in lawsuit against Trump officials
items.standfirst: Lawsuit claims conspiracy between top administrative officials and private groups to target the pro-Palestine advocate.
items.content: Lawsuit claims conspiracy between top administrative officials and private groups to target the pro-Palestine advocate.

items.title: Mahmoud Khalil files lawsuit accusing Trump officials, pro-Israel groups of conspiracy
items.standfirst: Mahmoud Khalil, a former Columbia University student who became the face of the Trump administration's crackdown on Palestinian rights demonstrators, filed a lawsuit in federal court Tuesday. The lawsuit alleges senior Trump administration officials, pro-Israel group the Heritage Foundation and online surveillance groups Canary Mission and Betar conspired against him.
items.content: Mahmoud Khalil, a former Columbia University student who became the face of the Trump administration's crackdown on Palestinian rights demonstrators, filed a lawsuit in federal court Tuesday. The lawsuit alleges senior Trump administration officials, pro-Israel group the Heritage Foundation and online surveillance groups Canary Mission and Betar conspired against him.

items.standfirst: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.
items.content: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.

clusters.title: China’s monthly car ‌exports top 1m for first time as overall trade soars
clusters.neutral_headline: China’s monthly car ‌exports top 1m for first time as overall trade soars
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: China’s monthly car ‌exports top 1m for first time as overall trade soars
items.standfirst: Country risks new tariffs from US and EU as it looks likely to match or beat last year’s record surplus of $1tnBusiness live – latest updatesChina’s monthly car ‌exports topped 1m for the first time in June as overall overseas shipments from the world’s second biggest economy rose 27%.Official Chinese customs data showed that a stronger-than-expected trade performance kept China on track to match or beat last year’s record trade surplus of $1tn (£748bn), achieved despite Donald Trump’s curtailed tariff war. Continue reading...
items.content: Country risks new tariffs from US and EU as it looks likely to match or beat last year’s record surplus of $1tnBusiness live – latest updatesChina’s monthly car ‌exports topped 1m for the first time in June as overall overseas shipments from the world’s second biggest economy rose 27%.Official Chinese customs data showed that a stronger-than-expected trade performance kept China on track to match or beat last year’s record trade surplus of $1tn (£748bn), achieved despite Donald Trump’s curtailed tariff war. Continue reading...

items.title: US refunds $81bn in Trump tariffs after supreme court ruled them illegal
items.standfirst: Government has been forced to pay back duties to companies that imported goods into the US that were hit by Trump’s tariffsChina’s monthly car ‌exports top 1m as overall trade soarsBusiness live – latest updatesThe US government has already paid back tens of billions of dollars in tariffs it collected before the supreme court ruled them illegal, according to budget figures released on Monday.Tariffs – taxes on imported goods – have been a key part of Donald Trump’s economic plan since he took office again last year. Continue reading...
